articles.headline: 'Finally': Norway star Erling Haaland on fulfilling World Cup dream
articles.description: Erling Haaland tells ESPN he is ready to make his World Cup debut and show his love for Norway on football's biggest stage.

images.name: Erling Haaland [1296x729]
images.caption: Erling Haaland has 55 goals in 50 games for Norway.
images.credit: Jared C. Tilton - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: World Cup legends and celebrities reveal their tournament predictions
images.caption: World Cup legends and celebrities reveal their tournament predictions

articles.headline: Jordan Henderson: Luka Modric is the best midfielder I've faced
articles.description: Jordan Henderson has praised Luka Modric as the greatest midfielder he has ever faced, ahead of England's match against Croatia on Wednesday.

images.name: Jordan Henderson Luka Modric [1296x729]
images.alt: Jordan Henderson challenges Luka Modric in the 2018 World Cup semifinal.

images.name: Henderson hails Modric as 'best' midfielder ahead of WC opener
images.caption: Henderson hails Modric as 'best' midfielder ahead of WC opener

articles.headline: World Cup Daily Live: New Zealand's match-predicting puppy poops on live TV
articles.description: Enjoy all the action from Monday's World Cup matches, with features, news, video analysis and color from around the competition.

images.name: Oli Sail, Tova O'Brien and Chris Chang with Bear the dog on TVNZ's Breakfast [1080x608]
Lone Curaçao supporter in Berlin celebrates goal vs. Germany
images.caption: Lone Curaçao supporter in Berlin celebrates goal vs. Germany

articles.headline: World Cup Last Dance: Courtois, Belgium's golden goalkeeper, gets one more go
articles.description: Belgium's 'Golden Generation' were the world's No. 1 team, but didn't win a trophy to show for it. Now, Thibaut Courtois is one of the last ones left.

images.name: Belgium's Thibaut Courtois World Cup 2026 portrait [1296x729]
images.credit: Steph Chambers - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Tielemans: Belgium coming to the World Cup to win
images.caption: Youri Tielemans is targeting World Cup glory as his Belgium side travel to their Seattle base camp.

articles.headline: Rod Stewart pictured at Scotland World Cup win shortly after cancelling concert due to illness
articles.description: Sir Rod Stewart was seen celebrating Scotland's World Cup win over Haiti at Gillette Stadium in Boston on Saturday night shortly after cancelling a concert due to illness.

images.name: Rod Stewart [1296x729]
images.alt: Rod Stewart celebrates at the game between Scotland and Haiti.
images.credit: (Photo by Martin Rickett/PA Images via Getty Images)

images.name: Scotland fans march to Fenway Park with bagpipes in Boston
images.caption: Scotland fans march to Fenway Park with bagpipes in Boston

articles.headline: Explained: How a chip in the ball allowed World Cup's fastest-ever goal by a sub to stand
articles.description: VAR overturned an offside decision after ruling there was a slight flick on the ball by Sweden's Alexander Isak, thanks to some technology in the ball.

images.name: Mattias Svanberg #19 of Sweden scores his team's fourth goal [1296x729]
images.caption: Mattias Svanberg #19 of Sweden scores his team's fourth goal
images.credit: Manuel Velasquez - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Potter: Isak-Gyokeres partnership showed quality in Tunisia win
images.caption: Potter: Isak-Gyokeres partnership showed quality in Tunisia win

competitions.altGameNote: FIFA World Cup, FIFA World Cup

article.headline: Netherlands and Mexico 0-0 at halftime
article.story: FORTALEZA, Brazil -- The Dutch were worried about the oppressive heat at Arena Castelao, and they left it with the stifling hot conditions to thank for a spot in the World Cup quarterfinals. An inspired tactical change during the second of two official stoppages led to a late goal from Netherlands forward Wesley Sneijder and an injury-time penalty from Klaas Jan Huntelaar in a dramatic 2-1 victory over Mexico on Sunday. "I moved to `Plan B' at the cooling break," said Netherlands coach Louis van Gaal, soon to be the manager at Manchester United. "That is a good way to take advantage of those breaks." Despite scoring 10 goals in three group matches, the Dutch again started the match with a defensive 5-3-2 system. Van Gaal made the switch to a more attacking 4-3-3 formation in the second half and tweaked it further when referee Pedro Proenca stopped the match in the 76th minute to allow players to rehydrate. That helped turn the match around and extend Mexico's second-round curse to six straight World Cups. "Unbelievable," said Arjen Robben, the Netherlands forward that earned the late penalty. "Five minutes from full time, we were out." Instead of the Dutch players, it was the Mexicans who wilted in the heat as they conceded twice as many goals in the final minutes of the match as they had in the entire group stage. "The humidity was against us, but we were fresher and fitter than the Mexicans," Van Gaal said. "Yes, we escaped. But we showed that we could create more chances with 4-3-3, and the players handled this shift very well." The Dutch will next face Costa Rica in the quarterfinals on Saturday in Salvador, and may have to play without midfielder Nigel de Jong, who was substituted in the ninth minute with a groin injury. Mexico coach Miguel Herrera, one of the most popular characters at the World Cup because of his emotive ways on the sidelines, blamed Proenca for the loss and accused Robben of diving under a challenge from Rafael Marquez to earn the penalty. "Today it was the man with the whistle who eliminated us from the World Cup," Herrera said. "If a referee invents a penalty, you're out of the World Cup. "I hope they have a look at what happened and that this gentleman goes home just like we are." Marquez said Robben admitted that it was a bad call, but added that the Dutchman thought he should have been awarded a spot kick for an earlier challenge. "I spoke with him after the match and he told me that it was not a penalty," Marquez said. "He said that the first foul was a penalty and that one was not called." Mexico has now advanced to the second round at the World Cup six straight times and not reached the quarterfinals. The last time the team made it that far was as host of the 1986 tournament. Giovani Dos Santos gave the Mexicans the lead in the 48th minute after a goalless first half, but Sneijder equalized with a hard shot in the 88th. It was the first time Sneijder, who scored five goals at the last World Cup in South Africa, has found the net in Brazil. Robben, who has already scored three goals for the Dutch at this year's tournament, then earned the penalty in the fourth minute of injury time but he handed the ball to Huntelaar. "You don't know when your chance will come, but it was today, and so you grab it with both hands," said Huntelaar, who replaced captain Robin van Persie in the 76th minute. "And it was fantastic." For Mexico, goalkeeper Guillermo Ochoa was again the star player, pulling off two more great saves after playing a key role in holding Brazil to a 0-0 draw in Group A. But he couldn't block Sneijder's shot and guessed wrong when diving in an attempt to stop Huntelaar's penalty. "It isn't easy to go out this way, the way this match went," Ochoa said. "It's just very hard to deal with when victory escapes you this way." --- Lineups: Netherlands: Jasper Cillessen; Paul Verhaegh (Memphis Depay, 56th), Daley Blind, Ron Vlaar, Stefan de Vrij, Dirk Kuyt; Nigel de Jong (Bruno Martins Indi, 9th), Georginio Wijnaldum, Wesley Sneijder; Arjen Robben, Robin van Persie (Klaas-Jan Huntelaar, 76th). Mexico: Guillermo Ochoa; Francisco Rodriguez, Rafael Marquez, Hector Moreno (Diego Reyes, 46th), Miguel Layun, Paul Aguilar; Carlos Salcido, Andres Guardado, Hector Herrera; Giovani Dos Santos (Javier Aquino, 61st), Oribe Peralta (Javier Hernandez, 75th).

keyEvents.text: Substitution, Netherlands. Bruno Martins Indi replaces Nigel de Jong because of an injury.
Substitution, Mexico. Diego Reyes replaces Héctor Moreno because of an injury.
Goal!  Netherlands 0, Mexico 1. Giovani dos Santos (Mexico) left footed shot from outside the box to the bottom right corner.
Substitution, Netherlands. Memphis Depay replaces Paul Verhaegh.
Substitution, Mexico. Javier Aquino replaces Giovani dos Santos.
Paul Aguilar (Mexico) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Mexico. Chicharito replaces Oribe Peralta.
Substitution, Netherlands. Klaas Jan Huntelaar replaces Robin van Persie.

Goal!  Netherlands 1, Mexico 1. Wesley Sneijder (Netherlands) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Klaas Jan Huntelaar with a headed pass   following a corner.
Rafael Márquez (Mexico) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Andrés Guardado (Mexico) is shown the yellow card.
Goal!  Netherlands 2, Mexico 1. Klaas Jan Huntelaar (Netherlands) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the bottom left corner.
